mnemonics.title: PSP Levels Memory Aid
mnemonics.content:
Level 0: Just Do It - Basic personal development
Level 1: Ticket-Based - Using tickets for work
Level 2: Personal Agile - Adding agile practices
Level 3: Personal Scrum - Full Scrum for individuals
Level 4: Optimize - Continuous improvement loop

mnemonics.title: PSP Phases
mnemonics.content:
P-Lan-ning: Estimate time and size
D-esign: Create design specs
C-ode: Implement the solution
T-est: Find and fix defects
P-ostmortem: Analyze and improve
Mnemonic: 'PLan, Design, Code, Test, Postmortem' = PLDCTP

mnemonics.title: TSP Roles
mnemonics.content:
Team Leader (TL) - Guides the team
Development Manager (DM) - Manages technical work
Quality Manager (QM) - Ensures quality standards
Mnemonic: 'The Leader Develops Quality' = TLDQ (Team Leader, Development, Quality)

acronyms.usage: Individual engineer's disciplined approach to software development focusing on personal process improvement
Team-based extension of PSP providing structure for team coordination and software delivery
Unit of measurement for code size used in calculating defect density
Technique for measuring project progress against the planned value in TSP
Process improvement model that inspired the level-based approach in PSP

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a staircase with 5 steps (0-4). Each step represents higher maturity: Level 0 is ground floor (ad-hoc), Level 4 is top floor (optimized, self-improving). Arrow points upward showing continuous improvement.
A circular cycle diagram: Planning → Design → Code → Test → Postmortem → (back to Planning for next project). The postmortem feeds into improved planning.
A triangle with Team Leader at top, Quality Manager and Development Manager below. All three point inward to the team goal in the center.
A simple formula: Defects ÷ Size (KLOC). Think of it like 'defects per square foot' - lower is better, like cleanliness.

analogies.analogy: Like a personal fitness tracker - you measure your workouts (time), track your form (quality), and analyze results to improve next time.
Like a student's report card - it tracks grades (metrics) over time to show improvement areas and academic growth.
Like a sports team - Coach (Team Leader), Captain (Development Manager), and Referee (Quality Manager) all work together but have different responsibilities.
Like a post-game analysis in sports - reviewing what strategies worked, what failed, and how to adjust for the next game.
